page.title: Marbella Feria 2028: Plan Your Yacht Charter for the Big Celebration

data.meta_description: Plan your Marbella Feria 2028 yacht charter from Puerto Banús. Book from €749/2h with skipper, drinks & VAT included. Secure your boat now.
data.summary: A practical guide to booking a yacht charter for the Marbella Feria 2028, with fleet options, pricing, and tips for enjoying the fair from the water.
key_facts: Marbella Feria 2028 runs for a week in early June (dates confirmed nearer the time).
All charters depart from Puerto Banús, just 5 minutes from the fairground.
Prices start at €749 for 2 hours on an Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 (skipper, drinks, VAT included).
Mangusta 80 variants (24m, 12 pax) include a free jet ski, minimum 4h at €4,719.
Every charter includes licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, soft drinks, beer, wine, cava, and snacks.
Book early: Feria week sees high demand, especially for larger yachts like Ferretti 94 or Canados 86.
Anchoring near the fair offers front-row views of fireworks and processions without the crowds.

faq.a: Exact dates for Marbella Feria 2028 are announced by the town hall around early 2028. Historically, the Feria starts the first or second week of June and lasts 6–7 days, with the main fairground (Recinto Ferial) open from midday until dawn. We recommend checking back from January 2028 for confirmed dates.

faq.q: How do I book a yacht charter for the Feria?
faq.a: You can book directly through our website by clicking the WhatsApp button on any boat page. For Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39), you can book online instantly. For all other yachts, we’ll confirm availability and send a quote within a few hours. Early booking is advised as Feria week sells out fast.

faq.q: Can we watch the Feria fireworks from the boat?
faq.a: Yes. Many of our charters anchor in the bay near the fairground, offering an unobstructed view of the nightly fireworks display. The Mangusta 80 and Azimut 58 Flybridge have spacious decks perfect for a group to watch the show while enjoying drinks and snacks.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, and Spanish IVA (21% VAT). There are no hidden fees. You only need to bring swimwear, sunscreen, and any extra food you’d like.

faq.q: How many people can we bring on a yacht?
faq.a: Our fleet ranges from 5 passengers (Dubhe, Mariah SX21) up to 12 passengers (Mangusta 80, Ferretti 94, Canados 86). For larger groups, we can arrange multiple boats. The maximum per boat is 12 passengers due to safety regulations.

faq.q: What is the best boat for a Feria party?
faq.a: For a lively group, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) is ideal with its large sundeck and free jet ski. The Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) offers a shaded flybridge and spacious saloon. For a budget-friendly option, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) is comfortable and well-equipped.

faq.q: Where do we depart from and what is the itinerary?
faq.a: All charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella. A typical Feria itinerary: cruise along the coast to anchor near the fairground, enjoy swimming and jet ski, watch the fireworks, then return. You can also visit Cabopino or Estepona if you prefer a quieter day.

faq.q: Can we cancel or change the booking?
faq.a: Yes. We offer free cancellation up to 14 days before departure. Changes to date or boat are subject to availability. For bookings made within 14 days of the charter, cancellation is not possible but we can reschedule if weather conditions are unsafe. Check our terms at booking.

data.body_html:
Marbella Feria 2028 is set to be the Costa del Sol’s most vibrant summer celebration, and there’s no better way to experience it than from the deck of a private yacht. Whether you want front-row seats to the nightly fireworks, a floating party base, or a peaceful escape from the crowds, a Marbella Feria 2028 yacht charter offers flexibility, comfort, and style. Departing directly from Puerto Banús, you can anchor within sight of the fairground, swim in calm Mediterranean waters, and enjoy premium service without the hassle of packed streets.

Why Charter a Yacht for Marbella Feria 2028?

The Feria de San Bernabé (commonly called Marbella Feria) transforms the town into a week-long fiesta with casetas, flamenco, live music, and spectacular fireworks. While the fairground is lively, it can get crowded, hot, and noisy. A yacht charter gives you a private sanctuary just offshore. You can watch the fireworks from your sundeck, anchor for a swim, and return to port whenever you wish. Plus, with drinks and snacks included, you avoid queueing at bars. For groups of 8 to 12, it’s often more cost-effective than buying multiple VIP tickets and drinks at the fair.

Our Fleet: Choose the Right Boat for Your Feria Experience

We operate a diverse fleet from Puerto Banús, ranging from compact speedboats to superyachts. All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, and Spanish IVA (21%). Here’s a quick overview of our most popular options for the Feria:

BoatLengthMax PaxStarting PriceHighlight
Astondoa 4012.5m9€749/2hBest value for small groups
Azimut 3912.5m11€749/2hSpacious cockpit for socialising
Mangusta 80 (White/Grey)24m12€4,719/4hFree jet ski & large sundeck
Azimut 58 Flybridge17.6m12On requestShaded flybridge, ideal for daytime
Ferretti 9429m12On requestLuxury interior, perfect for VIP groups

For other boats like the Canados 86, K80, or Pershing 46, pricing is available via WhatsApp. We recommend booking larger yachts (12 pax) at least 4–6 weeks in advance for Feria week.

How to Book Your Marbella Feria 2028 Yacht Charter

Booking is straightforward. For Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39), you can reserve instantly online with a deposit. For all other yachts, simply click the WhatsApp button on the boat’s page, tell us your preferred date, group size, and duration, and we’ll confirm availability within hours. We recommend booking as soon as the Feria dates are announced (usually by February 2028) to secure your first choice. You can also browse our full fleet to compare options.

Sample Feria Day Itinerary

Here’s a typical 4-hour charter schedule for a group of 10–12:

  • 18:00 – Board at Puerto Banús, meet skipper, safety briefing.
  • 18:15 – Departure, cruise along the coast towards the fairground area.
  • 18:45 – Anchor off Cala del Faro or near the Recinto Ferial. Swim, use the jet ski (on Mangusta 80), enjoy drinks and snacks.
  • 21:00 – Fireworks display begins. Watch from the deck.
  • 21:30 – Return to Puerto Banús. Optional: continue the evening at a beach club or caseta.

Longer charters (8 hours) allow visits to Cabopino or Estepona for lunch, then anchor near the Feria for the evening show.

What to Expect in Terms of Weather and Sea Conditions

June in Marbella averages 28–32°C air temperature and sea temperatures around 22–24°C. Winds are typically light (5–10 knots) from the west, making for calm seas. Afternoon sea breezes can pick up, but the sheltered bay near the fairground remains comfortable. We monitor weather closely; if conditions are unsafe, we’ll reschedule at no cost. Bring sunscreen, a hat, and a light jacket for the evening breeze.

Frequently Asked Questions

When is Marbella Feria 2028?

Exact dates for Marbella Feria 2028 are announced by the town hall around early 2028. Historically, the Feria starts the first or second week of June and lasts 6–7 days, with the main fairground (Recinto Ferial) open from midday until dawn. We recommend checking back from January 2028 for confirmed dates.

How do I book a yacht charter for the Feria?

You can book directly through our website by clicking the WhatsApp button on any boat page. For Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39), you can book online instantly. For all other yachts, we’ll confirm availability and send a quote within a few hours. Early booking is advised as Feria week sells out fast.

Can we watch the Feria fireworks from the boat?

Yes. Many of our charters anchor in the bay near the fairground, offering an unobstructed view of the nightly fireworks display. The Mangusta 80 and Azimut 58 Flybridge have spacious decks perfect for a group to watch the show while enjoying drinks and snacks.

What is included in the charter price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, and Spanish IVA (21% VAT). There are no hidden fees. You only need to bring swimwear, sunscreen, and any extra food you’d like.

How many people can we bring on a yacht?

Our fleet ranges from 5 passengers (Dubhe, Mariah SX21) up to 12 passengers (Mangusta 80, Ferretti 94, Canados 86). For larger groups, we can arrange multiple boats. The maximum per boat is 12 passengers due to safety regulations.

What is the best boat for a Feria party?

For a lively group, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) is ideal with its large sundeck and free jet ski. The Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) offers a shaded flybridge and spacious saloon. For a budget-friendly option, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) is comfortable and well-equipped.

Where do we depart from and what is the itinerary?

All charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella. A typical Feria itinerary: cruise along the coast to anchor near the fairground, enjoy swimming and jet ski, watch the fireworks, then return. You can also visit Cabopino or Estepona if you prefer a quieter day.

Can we cancel or change the booking?

Yes. We offer free cancellation up to 14 days before departure. Changes to date or boat are subject to availability. For bookings made within 14 days of the charter, cancellation is not possible but we can reschedule if weather conditions are unsafe. Check our terms at booking.

Ready to secure your spot for Marbella Feria 2028? Explore our full fleet and book your yacht charter today. For questions, reach out via WhatsApp — we’re here to help you plan the perfect celebration on the water.
